poem:
In the bare orchard, winter kept the keys.
The branches clicked them softly in the wind.
I walked the rows as if among old pleas,
where every leaf had vanished, but not pinned.
The earth still held the heat of buried rain.
It breathed through roots like memory through bone.
I knelt and found one stubborn green again,
a violet's fist in the split seam of stone.
So grief, I learned, is only rooted care,
a hand that cannot bear to let go soon.
It prunes the heart, then leaves a place for air,
and waits for light to enter after noon.
I rose with mud upon my cuff at eve,
and carried home the mercy to believe.
Yet at the gate my shadow crossed the creek:
Keep the small door open; let the dawn speak.
topic: the image "In the bare orchard, winter kept the keys."

poem:
The harbor lifts its lantern into light
And loosens smoke above the waking bay
Your sleeve still holds the weather of the bright
While gulls turn over roofs gone pale and gray
The tide keeps counting every board ashore
And salt stays where your hand had rested more

The rope unknots itself before the white
A skiff takes shape beside the patient quay
I hear your laugh still moving through the night
As if the dark were slow to leave the day
The pilings answer softly from the door
And tar keeps breathing from the planks below the floor

The east puts silver on the last of sight
A bell goes thin beyond the harbor spray
The moon withdraws but leaves a tender might
That drifts along the ropes and learns to sway
I stand and watch the channel open shore
And know the dawn can only ask for roar

So morning names what we have held from fright
And gives the gulls the whole unfinished play
Your absence moves across the water flight
Then breaks like mist and finally goes away
Yet still the pier remembers what it wore
And keeps your leaving in its grainy core
topic: a harbor parting at first light

poem:
At dawn I walked in harbor light
The tide withdrew from shore
Your scarf still held the taste of rain
One skiff moved out once more

The gulls climbed through the thinning bright
The bells woke near the door
The dock ropes thudded after chain
One skiff pulled free before

I raised my hand into the white
The wind came off the floor
It carried back your name through plain
One skiff kept time in roar

Now roofs take on the patient night
Day opens at the core
Yet salt remains as sharp again
Where you were mine, one oar
topic: a departure from the harbor wall

poem:
Dawn loosens silver on the bay
The fog gives back the waking day
A gull turns once beyond the glow
Its cry runs thin beneath the show

Your coat hangs pale against the gray
The dock rope taps a patient sway
The planks still shine beneath the low
And salt keeps speaking under snow

I watch one skiff push out to play
The oars divide the water's way
The bells go dim and travel slow
While smoke climbs straight in harbor crow

Now noon will claim the roofs and spray
Yet dawn still keeps a part to stay
The tide returns what losses know
And leaves your name in harbor throw
topic: a skiff leaving under morning fog

poem:
The harbor wakes beneath a patient light
And tarred ropes breathe the weather of the bay
A gull unseals the warehouse roof to bright
While one skiff rocks against the thinning gray

Your cup still warms the rail beside my sight
The tide keeps rubbing silver through the quay
The cranes stand still and take the dawn to white
As bells begin to measure out the day

I taste your leaving in the salt tonight
And hear it answer softly in the spray
The oars divide the dark with careful might
Then vanish where the channel learns to sway

So morning keeps what sorrow cannot fight
It opens every door that grief kept away
The harbor writes your name in broken flight
And leaves it where the waking waters say
topic: morning grief along a working quay

poem:
Dawn loosens silver into gray
Thin gulls turn over roofs all day
Your cup still warms beside the bay
Tide keeps one blue lantern alight

The ferry bell sounds low and lone
Wet pilings answer from the stone
I fold your scarf and stand alone
Tide keeps one blue lantern tonight

The east puts silver into light
The skiff slips outward into night
My breath goes after it in white
Tide keeps one blue lantern at night
topic: a lantern kept through a harbor goodbye

poem:
The harbor wakes beneath a wash of rain,
And ropes uncoil like writing in the gray;
I taste your leaving in the salt again,
While gulls go wheeling where the skiffs give way.
Your cup still warms the rail beside the bay.
The first light finds the place your hands let go,
And all the tide must keep, it keeps below.
topic: the harbor after someone leaves

poem:
The dock lamp trembles in the harbor lamp
Low rain keeps whispering over the tide
A cookfire settles slowly into ash

The cookfire settles slowly into ash
A watchman lifts his collar by the lamp
The pilings answer softly to the tide

The pilings answer softly to the tide
The watchman scuffs one boot through cooling ash
And waits for dawn beside the harbor lamp

Lamp, tide, ash keep the last watch.
topic: night watch at a rain-soaked harbor

poem:
Rain on tin roofs
Blue dusk under oaks
I go home alone
Slow crows are over our road
topic: a brief walk home at dusk

poem:
The harbor kept one lantern for the night
And tarred ropes breathed the weather of the bay
Your steps went quiet on the loading stone
I watched the skiff divide the final light
While gulls turned once above the waking day
And left my name to answer back alone
The tide took hold of every loosened spar
The east unstitched the dark above the gray
A bell rang low beneath the warehouse tone
I kept your cup and learned what mornings are
Dawn leaves me known
topic: a harbor after someone leaves before dawn

poem:
The harbor opens slowly into light
Your cup still warms beside the weathered quay
The wet rope slaps a patient note of gray
I hear your leaving in the broken flight
The tide keeps silver on the splintered white
A bell goes thin beyond the ferry day
The harbor opens slowly into sight
Your cup still warms beside the weathered bay
The skiff moves outward with a careful slight
Its oars keep time against the waking spray
My breath stays low beneath the thinning sway
And salt returns to everything with might
The harbor opens slowly into night
topic: a harbor parting carried by the morning tide

poem:
The harbor keeps one lantern in the light
Your cup still warms beside the waking quay
The gulls turn once and vanish into night
The tide keeps speaking under sheets of gray

A wet rope taps the rail and will not sway
I watch one skiff pull slowly into bright
The dock boards breathe the weather of the bay
The harbor keeps one lantern in the white

The moon lets go of every borrowed flight
The cranes stand still above the cargo day
My hands remember how your hands were slight
Your cup still warms beside the waking spray

The east unthreads the smoke and thin array
The oars divide the channel with their might
A bell goes far beyond the ferry play
The gulls turn once and vanish into sight

Now dawn puts silver on the pilings' height
And salt returns in small deliberate stay
I keep your name against the coming starlight
The tide keeps speaking under sheets of clay

So morning takes the roofs from patient away
Yet leaves one hush that glimmers into twilight
The harbor opens where we used to pray
And carries all our parting toward daylight
topic: a harbor vigil stretched across the whole night

poem:
The harbor keeps one lantern for the night
Damp ropes exhale their weather on the quay
A gull turns once and disappears from sight
Thin smoke unthreads itself into the gray
Your cup still warms the rail where your hands lay
I touch its rim and hear the mooring stone
The tide repeats what I could never say
Stand by the pier until the bells are known

The east begins to trouble with a light
The cranes wake up like iron crows at bay
One skiff moves out and cuts the moon to white
Its oars keep time beneath a skin of spray
The channel opens where your leaving lay
My hands remember yours and then their own
A bell goes thin beyond the customs way
Stand by the pier until the bells are known

Now roofs take on the patience of full light
The market doors unlatch the salted day
Yet every plank still holds the parting night
And gives it back in splinters of the gray
I learn how grief can labor through the day
It lifts no cry; it speaks through tar and stone
It asks me only not to turn away
Stand by the pier until the bells are known

Prince of the heart, keep faith with common day
Let salt and wind make room in flesh and bone
Stay where first sorrow learned its human sway
Stand by the pier until the bells are known
topic: a harbor vigil held through the turning of dawn

poem:
Before first gulls, the harbor cups a wick of light
Wet ropes give back the smell of iron at the quay
Your footstep lingers in the chill along the stone
Thin smoke from bait sheds braids itself into the gray
I keep your cup as if it still contained the night
The mastheads answer one another with a tone
And every shutter in the market waits alone
A weather bell keeps counting through the rinsing rain
The tide drags pebbles down the slip in a slow chain
Far windows on the hill begin to warm and glow
One skiff unhooks itself and noses out below
I want to call you back, but salt consumes the fire
And leaves your name alive along the signal wire

By sunrise every warehouse edge is cut in bright
The fishers trade their jokes beside the widening bay
I watch a gull lift cleanly with a mussel bone
The ferryman steps lightly through the opening day
Your absence has the clean and cutting weight of sight
It shows me what I knew and had not wholly known
That love is built from work the hands perform on their own
The winches cough awake and grind the nets like grain
A dray rolls over ruts and rattles toward the train
The children on the bluff invent a laughing show
Even the black tar roofs take on a sift of snow
By noon the steeple seems to take the whole sky higher
And from the eaves a flock lifts like a loosened choir

By noon the channel burns with hammered sheets of white
Two tugboats stitch their wakes across the salted spray
Engines beneath the pier keep up a patient drone
The lunchroom door swings out and bangs itself away
The heat climbs every ladder rung toward blinding height
Still every board repeats the promise you had shown
The black tide under pilings keeps its inward moan
A cart of ice goes creaking slowly down the lane
Then comes back empty, dripping through the yard again
From open tar the heat draws up a darkened flow
And every keel knocks softly in the undertow
A radio in the bait shop threads one thin desire
Beside a rusted bicycle and its hanging tire

By afternoon the cranes cast bars that mimic flight
The clerk across the square keeps ledgers on a tray
Each crate arrives from ports beyond our weathered zone
Small boys chase bottlecaps and scatter them in play
I hear your laugh returning in the angled daylight
It feels as if your voice has only briefly flown
Not lost, but planted where the deepest things are sown
A cloudbank bruises slowly and darkens every pane
Then passes, leaving silver on the channel main
The first lamps in the chandlery burn patient and low
And barges nudge the pilings with a patient sideways throw
A match is struck beneath a kettle, small and dire
While swallows tangle in the rigging like a briar

By evening all the western panes are full of moonlight
The market closes early where the day's last shadows lay
One woman folds the awning with a patient groan
The ferries sound and shoulder through the last long way
What leaves us always sharpens at the edge of twilight
Yet something in the boards declares this grief our loan
The tide repeats it softly until it seems full grown
The lamps come on one after one and make a golden stain
And every length of water gathers them in refrain
The sheds fall dark enough that only one bird dares to crow
The moored boats settle softly to the patience of their tow
I take your cup and know this waiting has made me entire
Not healed, but steadier, and no longer sunk in mire

So let the night keep some small part of what stays unknown
And let the bell complete its weathered work and remain
I walk home slowly while your silence takes a patient throne
The roofs hold back the dark until they pulse with violet vein
And what the harbor teaches will not turn to overblown
topic: one harbor day lived inside the memory of a parting
